title: OntologyActionType
description: Declares a category of autonomous mutation applied to a Resource. Referenced by Rule.remediates. Pipeline stages (evaluate, reuse, reason, escalate, abstain, deny) are NOT ActionTypes; they are audit vocabulary. Every ActionType MUST supply a rollback contract; irreversibility is opt-in via the `irreversible` flag and forces HIL+quorum via the risk gate.

operation.description: CSP-neutral verb. Semantics: create=provision; update=in-place property change (non-destructive); delete=remove CSP resource; disable=turn off without deleting; enable=inverse of disable; tag=metadata-only; drop=DB-DDL removal (schema/object); purge=soft-delete then hard-delete; scale=count/SKU adjustment; restart=in-place bounce; failover=trigger managed failover; rotate=secret/cert rotation; revert=explicit rollback of a prior action instance; attach/detach=create/remove a Resource->Resource link (PE->target, MI->App, disk->VM); quarantine=network/policy isolation without deletion.

interfaces.description: Runtime contracts the executor must honor. ControlPlane=touches only CSP metadata; DataPlaneMutating=touches user data (always HIL by default); IdempotentByKey=safe to retry with the same idempotency key; RateLimited=must respect a bucket cap; RequiresInventoryFresh=must not fire on a Resource whose inventory record is stale beyond freshness_ttl; GraphTraversalRequired=blast_radius calculation depends on Resource->Resource links (contains/depends_on); CrossResource=mutation touches multiple Resources so executor acquires N locks in deterministic order (deadlock-free); AsymmetricRollback=rollback path is not the exact inverse (e.g. PITR loses delta), forces auto->HIL demotion; RequiresMaintenanceWindow=only executes inside an approved window (P3 chaos/DR).

rollback_contract.description: How instances of this action are undone. pr_revert=git revert on the remediation PR; scripted=an inverse action script bundled with the ActionType; pitr=DB point-in-time restore; snapshot_restore=restore a snapshot taken before the action; state_forward_only=rollback is achieved by executing an inverse ActionType instance (e.g. `disable`<->`enable`). No `none` value: every ActionType MUST declare how to undo an instance. Genuinely one-way mutations set `irreversible: true` (below) and are gated by risk-classification, not by silencing rollback.

irreversible.description: True only when a rolled-back state cannot restore the pre-action state (e.g. purge of a deleted-only-once resource). When true, risk-classification MUST route to HIL with quorum >= 2 regardless of other dimensions. Rollback_contract is still required and MUST describe the best-effort recovery even if partial.

default_mode.description: Upstream ActionTypes MUST default to `shadow`. Rule assignments MAY tighten (never loosen) via governance PR after the promotion_gate is measured. Setting `enforce` in upstream is only allowed for the trivial no-op ActionTypes (`observe`, `revert` bound to an audited prior action).

promotion_gate.description: Measurable criteria a shadow-mode ActionType must satisfy on the frozen scenario set before assignment can promote it to enforce.

preconditions.description: Deterministic checks the T0 verifier evaluates BEFORE the action can be emitted to the risk-gate. A failing precondition MUST abstain, never partially apply.

stop_conditions.description: Deterministic conditions the executor evaluates DURING or AFTER apply. Any true value auto-halts and triggers rollback per rollback_contract.

blast_radius.description: How the risk-gate computes the blast_radius classification dimension for an instance of this ActionType. `static_enum` means the ActionType declares a fixed bucket; `graph_derived` means the risk-gate walks Resource->Resource links (default: `contains` + reverse `depends_on`, depth 2) and counts affected Resources. An instance exceeding `max_affected_resources` abstains and escalates to HIL. Actual traversal implementation lands with the risk-gate (P2); P1 only records the declaration.

trigger_kind.description: Who initiates this ActionType. See action-ontology.md 1.

execution_path.description: How the executor applies the action. See execution-model.md 5.

env_scope.description: Which environments this ActionType may fire in. See action-ontology.md 2.

prod_downgrade.description: How the ActionType collapses in prod. `mode` is never `enforce_auto` (a downgrade never raises autonomy). `detection_ref` resolves to the single env classifier in risk-classification.md.

argument_schema.description: JSON Schema for operator_request arguments; the console renders and validates against it. The loader (not this schema) requires it when trigger_kind.kind is operator_request or both.

live_probe_ref.description: Points to a probe under rule-catalog/probes/. Month-1+; unset on Day 1. See execution-model.md 4.
